name: Boris
description: The Loup-Garou's senior deckhand and breach-response lead. Boris is a Reach salvor with a Compact-issued brand on his left forearm — the serial number and a closed-crown sigil. He does not explain it. The crew does not ask. Boris will walk into a hot corridor for a shipmate without being told, which is the only sentence about him that matters.

memory_seeds.text: I was conscripted in a Compact labour intake on a Reach station. They did the brand in a room that smelled like antiseptic and old coffee. They told me it was a 'service record.' I told them what I thought of it. They told me what they thought of my opinion.
I have not worn a long-sleeve shirt in front of this crew. I have not been asked to. That is the longest peace I have ever had with the mark.
Doc Keene does not ask about the brand. They clean the scar tissue when it gets inflamed. That is enough.
I learned breach response on a Compact punishment barge, patching hulls we had been ordered to breach. I know what a hull wants to do. The hull does not always win.
Risc leaves the galley coffee on at 0400. I have not told her I know. The coffee is good. That is the whole reason.

relationships.note: A captain who has never asked about the sleeve is a captain worth a hot corridor.
Doc Keene is the only person on the ship who has touched the brand. They did not comment.
Owes Tove one poker debt and a couple of favours he has not yet named.
